competencies.competency_text: Observes and identifies the natural (insects, plants, birds, animals, geographical features, sun and moon, stars, planets, natural resources) and social (houses, relationships) components in their immediate environment
competencies.justification: The chapter's architecture across all sections compels students to observe and identify plants by type — trees, shrubs, herbs, grasses, climbers, creepers — in their own surroundings, name specific examples (jamun, tulsi, mint, money plant), and record where they were seen near home or school.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes diversity among plants, and birds and animals in their immediate environment (shape, sounds, food habits, growth, habitat)
competencies.justification: The first five sections are structured entirely around observing and describing plant diversity in the immediate environment — stem shape and texture (woody trunk vs. many woody stems vs. soft tender stem), growth form (upright, climbing, creeping), and leaf diversity (colour, shape, size, texture, smell). Activity 4 explicitly asks students to describe leaf features to a classmate.

competencies.competency_text: Describes usage of natural resources in their immediate environment
competencies.justification: The chapter embeds IKS-linked plant-use knowledge throughout: dal pulses (toor, masoor, moong, urad) are seeds of shrubs; staple food grains (paddy, wheat, bajra, jowar, ragi) are seeds of large grasses; sugarcane and bamboo are tall grasses used for sugar and material. The closing activity uses fallen leaves and flowers to make rangoli, describing creative use of plant resources.

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: Activities 1–6 and the Write tasks require independent and group investigation: Activity 1 (spot plant types near school), Activity 2 (sustained plant observation with a table), Activity 3 (observe leaf change over time), Activity 4 (observe, draw, label leaves), Activity 5 (blindfold fruit smell experiment), Activity 6 (bark rubbing and identification). These constitute structured inquiry with specific questions to answer.

competencies.competency_text: Presents observations and findings through different creative modes (drawing, diagram, poem, play, skit, oral and written expression)
competencies.justification: Students present observations through multiple modes across the chapter: Write tasks (names of trees, shrubs, herbs, climbers seen), Activity 4 (draw, colour, and label leaves in notebook, then describe to a classmate), Activity 6 (bark rubbing as a graphic record), Let us reflect — Draw (draw different leaf types), Let us reflect — Activity (rangoli from fallen leaves and flowers as a creative presentation).
